title: oxyacantha × macrocarpa
intro: Oxyacantha × macrocarpa, also known as the hawthorn tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The medicinal properties of Oxyacantha × macrocarpa lie in its berries, leaves, and flowers, which are rich in flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Oxyacantha × macrocarpa include reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and enhancing the overall function of the heart, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Oxyacantha × macrocarpa is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in combination with other herbs in various herbal preparations.
